topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the old park bench felt suddenly cold beneath my fingertips. I was just taking a break, nursing a lukewarm coffee, when I spotted it tucked beneath the slats. A wallet. As I picked it up, my hand froze mid-air. It was heavy, far heavier than it should have been, and when I flipped it open, the sight of the bills inside made my breath hitch. A thick stack of cash, enough to buy a small house, spilled out onto the dusty ground. My eyes darted from the money to the empty space where the owner must have been. I stood there, rooted to the spot, my mouth slightly agape, unable to process the sheer volume of it.

[story 2]

It was a mundane Tuesday afternoon, the kind where nothing ever happens. Then, while sweeping the seldom-used corner of the market, my broom nudged something metallic. A wallet. My heart gave a sudden, hard thump against my ribs. When I finally managed to pry it open, the reality hit me like a physical blow. The sheer quantity of currency inside made my hands tremble uncontrollably. I dropped the wallet onto the wooden floor, staring down at the vibrant green and brown paper, my gaze flickering back and forth between the cash and the empty aisle. I couldn't seem to form a coherent thought; just a wide-eyed, silent paralysis settled over me.

[story 3]

I found it lying near the entrance to the library—a leather wallet carelessly dropped. My initial curiosity quickly morphed into something far more intense as I examined its contents. When I counted the bundles, the numbers swam before my eyes. Each note felt significant, weighty in my palm. I sat down heavily on the stone steps, the sound of my own quickened breathing echoing in the quiet space. I slowly ran my thumb over the edges of the cash, feeling the crispness of the paper, a strange, dizzying sensation washing over me. I leaned back, my jaw slack, utterly unable to move or speak, just absorbing the impossible amount resting there.
